pr.title: Skills: per-domain structure, policy README, and domain scaffolds
pr.description:
## Summary

Restructures `.agents/skills/` from one catch-all skill into a per-domain layout, so operational knowledge scales with the engines/components it belongs to and can later feed per-domain Atlas graphics (skills, parameters, checklists, tests per domain).

**Policy (`.agents/skills/README.md`):**
- Skills = how to operate/test a domain; capability state/trust stays in registries (skills reference, never duplicate counts).
- Standard scannable sections per SKILL.md: Domain / Commands / Registry pointers / Verification checklists / Tests / Notes.
- **Lifecycle rule:** any PR changing how a domain is operated/tested must update that domain's SKILL.md in the same PR, populated at the end before marking ready â€” no separate one-off skill PRs.

**Split (content moved, not rewritten):** `testing-axiom-cli` (was ~270 lines covering everything) is now the base recordable-terminal skill; its checklists moved to sibling skills:
- `axiom-pr-workflow` â€” compute mode, pre-review self-audit, Purpose-to-Workflow gate, tiering, reporting
- `testing-execution-chain` â€” M4 execution-chain-run checklists
- `testing-evidence-promotion` â€” M2 capability-evidence-apply (incl. Finding-2 EMPTY quarantine step) + cli-validation-record
- `testing-cli-governance` â€” runner-commands + framework create/show/export pattern
- `testing-atlas-ui` â€” Atlas UI checklist (incl. static-snapshot restart gotcha)
- `testing-local-runner` â€” runner task format, allowlisted actions, emit_evidence_summary, WDAC notes

**Scaffolds** (`status: scaffold`, standard sections, verified pointers only): `agents-orchestration`, `revit-adapter`, `inventory-discovery`, `prompt-resolver`.

## What did not change
No runtime code, tests, or CI. Docs-only (tier 1 â€” full pytest intentionally not run).

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

Split skills into per-domain structure with skills policy (README) and scaffolds
